text.title: Shadow Handover Session Decision Tree
text.summary: Tree mapping observable signals (outgoing-availability, task-list readiness, drive verdicts, sign-off captured, spf items) to run / second-session / forensic-fallback.

decision-tree.root-question: outgoing_engineer available and willing for at least one session?

conclusion.verdict: skip-this-methodology; use forensic-handover artefacts (postmortems, runbooks, code-archaeology)
defer; agree task list with both parties before scheduling session

branch.when: outgoing_unavailable == false AND task_list_missing == false AND drove_violation == true

conclusion.verdict: reject session record; schedule second session; outgoing engineer cannot drive non-recovery tasks

branch.when: outgoing_unavailable == false AND task_list_missing == false AND drove_violation == false AND sign_off_missing == true

conclusion.verdict: reject record; schedule sign-off slot; rotation date gated

branch.when: outgoing_unavailable == false AND task_list_missing == false AND drove_violation == false AND sign_off_missing == false AND spf_present == true

conclusion.verdict: defer rotation date until SPF items have doc_link OR second-session scheduled

branch.when: outgoing_unavailable == false AND task_list_missing == false AND drove_violation == false AND sign_off_missing == false AND spf_present == false

conclusion.verdict: accept record; convert open gap-log items to doc tickets within 1 week
preconditions not met; route to correct methodology or skip.

text.example: outgoing=available → task_list=5 tasks → all drove or deferred → both signed → spf list empty → accept; open gap_log items → doc tickets.
